title: AN ACT DECLARING AUGUST 21 OF EVERY YEAR, AS NINOY AQUINO DAY, A SPECIAL NONWORKING HOLIDAY, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES

chunks.text:
S. No. 2636 H. No. 6240 / Malaya 3/3/04 ; Today 3/3/04

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 9256, February 25, 2004 ]

AN ACT DECLARING AUGUST 21 OF EVERY YEAR, AS NINOY AQUINO DAY, A SPECIAL NONWORKING HOLIDAY, AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1.

Ninoy Aquino Day

. 芒聙聰 In order to commemorate the death anniversary of former Senator Benigno "Ninoy" S. Aquino, Jr., August twenty-one of every year is hereby declared as the Ninoy Aquino day which shall be a national nonworking holiday.

SEC. 2.

Edsa Commission.

芒聙聰 The EDSA Commission created pursuant to Executive Order No. 82 (s. 1999) shall plan and implement appropriate ceremonies for the observance of the Ninoy Aquino Day.

SEC. 3.

Funding

. 芒聙聰 The operations of the EDSA Commission shall be funded from the Budget of the Office of the President and from private donations. Funds accruing to and expenses made by the EDSA Commission shall be subject to the usual accounting rules and procedures.

SEC. 4.

Repealing Clause.

芒聙聰 All laws, presidential decrees, orders, issuances, rules and regulations and any part thereof inconsistent, which are inconsistent with provisions of this Act are hereby amended or modified accordingly.

SEC. 5.

Separability Clause

. 芒聙聰 If any provision of this Act is held unconstitutional or invalid, all other provisions not affected thereby shall remain valid.

SEC. 6.

Effectivity.

芒聙聰 This Act shall take effect fifteen (15) days after its publication in at least two (2) newspapers of general circulation.

Approved,

(Sgd.) FRANKLIN M. DRILON

(Sgd.) JOSE DE VENECIA JR.

President of the Senate

Speaker of the House of Representatives

This Act, which is a consolidation of Senate Bill No. 2636 and House

Bill No. 6240, was finally passed by the Senate and the House of

Representatives on November 18, 2003 and January 26, 2004, respectively.

(Sgd.) OSCAR G. YABES

(Sgd.) ROBERTO P. NAZARENO

Secretary of the Senate

Secretary General

House of Representatives

Approved: FEB 25 2004

(Sgd.) GLORIA MACAPAGAL - ARROYO

President of the Philippines
